sections.title: Bylaws Family Practice & Counseling Services Network
Article I: Mission and Activities
sections.content:
Mission. Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) provides person-centered, integrated and comprehensive health services to individuals and families across their lifespan. FPCSN promotes an antiracist, trauma-informed culture, as well as resiliency and well-being among patients, staff and surrounding communities.
Activities. The activities of the Corporation shall be those necessary and appropriate to accomplish the purposes of the Corporation as stated in these Bylaws and in the Articles of Incorporation.

sections.title: Article II: Offices
sections.content:
Offices. The principal office of the Corporation shall be 4700 Wissahickon Avenue, Suite 118, Philadelphia, PA 19144 or such other place as shall be designated by the Board of Directors.
Article III: Board of Directors
Number and Composition
The Board of Directors will consist of at least nine (9) but not more than twenty-five (25) members.
Composition. The Board of Directors shall have the following composition:
a) At least fifty-one percent (51%) of the directors shall be patients served by the Corporation's health center and who, as a group, demographically represent the individuals served by the health center and should include representation from public housing. A patient is an individual who has received at least one service from the health center in the past twenty-four (24) months that generated a health center visit, where both the service and the site where the service was received are with the health center's scope of project as approved by the Health Resources and Services Administration.
b) No more than fifty percent (50%) of the remaining directors may be individuals who derive more than ten percent (10%) of their annual income from the health care industry. The remaining directors shall be representative of the community in which the Corporation's health center is located, including public housing and shall be selected for their expertise in community affairs, local government, finance and banking, legal affairs, and other commercial and industrial concerns, or social service agencies within the community.
c) The Chief Executive Officer may only serve as a non-voting, ex-officio member of the Board.
d) Qualifications: All Board members shall meet the following additional minimal qualifications:
a. Members shall be residents of Philadelphia or state of PA and at least eighteen (21)
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530
Page Number: 66 Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM
EHB Application Number: 242645 Grant Number: N/A Docusign Envelope ID: DA986F84-D034-4883-85C0-FFDA2F3166FF
years old.
b. No Member shall be FPCSN employee, or an immediate family member (i.e., spouse, child, parent, brother, or sister by blood, adoption, or marriage) of such employee.
c. The legal guardian of a patient who is a dependent patient or adult, or a legal sponsor of an immigrant, may be considered a health center patient for purposes of Board representation
d. Members shall participate in appropriate training and educational programs necessary to properly fulfill their responsibilities as Board Members.
e. Consumer Members must be a current registered health center patient and must have accessed the health center in the past 24 months to receive at least one or more in-scope service(s) that generate a health center visit.
Powers of Board of Directors. Subject to the limitation of law, or the Articles of Incorporation and of these Bylaws, all corporate powers shall be exercised by or under authority of, and the business and affairs of the Corporation shall be controlled by a Board of Directors.
The Board of Directors' specific duties and authorities shall include:
a) Approving the selection, and termination or dismissal of the health center's Chief Executive Officer;
b) Approving the health center's program project budget and applications;
c) Approving the health center's services and locations and hours of operations.
d) Evaluating the health center's performance, including
a. Achievement of project objectives.
b. Service utilization patterns.
c. Quality of care.
d. Efficiency and effectiveness of the health center
e) Establishing or adopting policy related to the operations of the health center; and
f) Assuring the health center operates in compliance with applicable Federal, State, and local laws and regulations
g) Prohibit conflict of interest or appearance of the same by Members, employees, consultants, and those who provide services or goods to the CHC.
Election; Term of Office. The Directors shall be elected by a vote of the majority of the Directors at a Board meeting.
There is no term of office for FPCSN Board of Directors.
Vacancies. Any vacancy occurring in the Board of Directors may be filled by the Board of Directors or, if the directors remaining in office constitute less than a quorum, by the affirmative vote of a majority of the remaining directors. Candidates shall be nominated by the directors remaining in office. No reduction of the number of directors shall have the effect of removing any director prior to the expiration of their term of office.
Removal of Directors. Any individual director may be removed from office, with or without cause, by a vote of two-thirds (2/3) of the directors then in office. If a director misses three
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530
Page Number: 67 Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM
EHB Application Number: 242645 Grant Number: N/A
Docusign Envelope ID: DA986F84-D034-4883-85C0-FFDA2F3166FF
consecutive regular meetings without notice to the Chairperson within twenty-four (24) hours prior to the scheduled meeting, they may be removed by a vote of a majority of the directors then in office.
Regular Meetings. Meetings of the Board of Directors shall be held at least once a month or at such other intervals as determined by the Board of Directors.
Special Meetings. Special meetings of the Board of Directors for any purpose shall be called at any time by the Chairperson or by at least twenty percent (20%) of the directors then in office.
Place of Meetings. Meetings of the Board of Directors shall be held at the principal office of the Corporation or virtually.
Notice of Meetings. Meetings of the Board of Directors may be held without notice, unless as otherwise provided herein.
Any Board of Directors action to remove a director or to approve matters concerning:
a) director's conflict of interest.
b) indemnification.
c) amendment of the Corporation's Articles of Incorporation.
d) a plan of merger or conversion.
e) the sale or mortgage of all or substantially all of the Corporation's assets; or
f) dissolution: shall not be valid unless each director is given at least seven (7) days written notice that the matter will be voted upon at a Board of Directors meeting, unless notice is waived in writing, signed by the director so waiving notice, and filed with the Corporation's minutes.
Action Without a Meeting. Any action required or permitted to be taken by the Board of Directors at a meeting may be taken without a meeting if all directors (i) execute a consent in writing, setting forth the action to be taken, or (ii) transmit an electronic transmission consenting to the action to be taken. The consent shall be included in the minutes or filed with the corporate records reflecting the action taken. Action taken under this Section is effective when the last director signs the consent or gives consent by electronic transmission unless the consent specifies a different effective date.
Participation in Meetings through Electronic Communications. Members of the Board of Directors of the Corporation, or any committee designed by such Board of Directors may participate in a meeting of such Board of Directors or committee through the use of any means of communication including in person or virtual whereby all persons participating in the meeting can simultaneously hear each other, and participation in a meeting in such manner shall constitute presence in person at such meeting.
Quorum and Vote. Fifty-one percent of the current members of the Board of Directors in office immediately before a meeting begins shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business. When a quorum is present, the affirmative votes of a majority of the directors present at any meeting are required to approve a corporate act. In the absence of a quorum,
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530 Page Number: 68
Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM
EHB Application Number: 242645 Grant Number: N/A Docusign Envelope ID: DA986F84-D034-4883-85C0-FFDA2F3166FF
directors may meet from time to time to discuss matters under consideration by the Board of Directors, but may not transact any business. No proxies shall be allowed.
Compensation. Board members of all committees shall not receive any salary or other compensation for their services as such.
Board Committees. In addition to the standing committees set forth below, by resolution adopted by a majority of the directors in office, the Board of Directors may designate and appoint one or more Board committees and appoint members of the Board of Directors to serve on them. Such committees, to the extent provided in the resolution, shall have such powers and authority as the Board of Directors shall delegate to it, except that no such committee shall have the authority of the Board of Directors in reference to
a) amending, altering or repealing the By-Laws,
b) electing, appointing or removing any member of any committee or any director or officer of the Corporation,
c) amending the Articles of Incorporation, restating the Articles of Incorporation,
d) authorizing the sale, lease, exchange or mortgage of all or substantially all of the property and assets of the Corporation,
e) authorizing the voluntary dissolution of the Corporation or revoking proceedings therefore;
f) adopting a plan for the distribution of the assets of the Corporation, or
g) amending, altering or repealing any resolution of the Board of Directors. All directors are expected to serve on at least one committee.
Standing Committees. The Board of Directors shall have the following standing committees:
a) Executive Committee. The Executive Committee shall be comprised of the officers of the Corporation elected pursuant to Article V. Subject to any limitations imposed by the Board of Directors, the Executive Committee shall have the power to act on behalf of the entire Board regarding issues which arise during the period between meetings of the Board of Directors. Seventy-five percent of the members of the Executive Committee shall constitute a quorum necessary for transaction of business. If a quorum is present, the affirmative votes of a majority of the members voting on any matter are required to approve any action taken by the Executive Committee on behalf of the Board of Directors.
b) Finance/Audit Committee. The Finance/Audit Committee shall be comprised of the Treasurer and at least one other director. The Finance/Audit Committee shall oversee the fiscal and accounting matters of the Corporation and the audit of the Corporation. The Finance/Audit Committee shall also perform such other appropriate duties as may be prescribed by the Board.
c) Quality Assurance Committee. The Quality Assurance Committee shall be comprised of at least two directors. The Quality Assurance Committee shall monitor and evaluate patient care and identify, study and recommend improvements in the Corporation's patient care delivery process. The Quality Assurance Committee shall also perform such other appropriate duties as may be prescribed by the Board.
Code of Ethics/Conflict of Interest. A strict code of ethics shall exist with the Board of
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530 Page Number: 69 Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM
EHB Application Number: 242645 Grant Number: N/A Docusign Envelope ID: DA986F84-D034-4883-85C0-FFDA2F3166FF
Directors. Directors are expected to abide by the same Code of Conduct that applies to members of Family Practices and Counseling Network Services Health Center's workforce and disclose any potential conflict of interest prior to participating in the Board of Director's deliberations on any matter in which they may have a personal financial interest. No one may become a director solely for the purpose of furthering his or her individual business or organizational interest. In order to avoid any appearance of any conflict of interest, no director will be permitted to vote or influence the remainder of the directorship vote in his or her behalf on any matter, which would serve his or her individual interest.
Disclosure. The real and potential interests of any director in any financial transaction, contract or agreement arising in the course of affairs of the Corporation shall be fully disclosed to the Board of Directors at the earliest opportunity or practicable date, as set forth in the conflict-of-interest policy adopted by the Board of Directors.
Article V. Officers Designation. The officers of the Corporation shall be a Chairperson, a Secretary and a Treasurer and such other officers as may be designated by the Board of Directors.
Election and Term of Office. Officers shall be elected by members of the Board of Directors at the Annual Meeting or at a special meeting called for that purpose and installed as the first order of business to assume office for the remainder of the meeting. Term of office shall begin with the meeting at which such election occurs and shall be for three (3) years. An officer who has completed two (2) consecutive terms in the same office shall not be re-elected to that office of the ensuing year. No third party may appoint a majority of the total number of health center Board members or a majority of the non-patient members.
Removal and Resignation. Any officer may be removed, with or without cause, by the Board of Directors at any regular or special meeting. Any officer may resign at any time by giving written notice to the Board of Directors or to the Chairperson or to the Secretary of the Corporation. Any such resignation shall take effect at the date of the receipt of such notice or at any later time specified therein, and, unless otherwise specified therein, the acceptance of such resignation shall not be necessary to make it effective. No third party may require the removal of any current Board member not appointment by that party.
Vacancies. Any vacancy in any office because of death, resignation, removal, disqualification or other cause shall be filled in the manner prescribed in these By-Laws for regular appointments to such office.
The Chairperson. The Chairperson shall be selected from the Board of Directors and shall preside over all meetings of the Board of Directors. They shall be kept advised of the general affairs of the Corporation and ensure that all orders, resolutions and policies of the Board of Directors are carried out into effect. They shall chair the Executive Committee and serve as ex-officio member of all other committees. They shall have the usual power and duties customarily vested in the office of the Chairperson. If for any reason it becomes necessary to replace the Chairperson prior to expiration of their term, the Treasurer shall fill the unexpired term, and the Board of Directors shall elect a new Treasurer.
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530 Page Number: 70 Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM
EHB Application Number: 242645 Grant Number: N/A Docusign Envelope ID: DA986F84-D034-4883-85C0-FFDA2F3166FF
Secretary. The Secretary shall keep, or cause to be kept, a book of minutes, at the principal office or such other place as the Board of Directors may order, of all meetings of directors, with the time and place of holding, whether regular or special, and if special, how authorized the notice thereof given, the names of those present at directors meeting and the proceeding thereof. The Secretary shall keep, or cause to be kept, at the principal office of the Corporation a copy of the Articles of incorporation and By-Laws. The Secretary shall keep a record of the Corporation's directors, showing each director's name and address. The Secretary shall give, or cause to be given, notice of all meetings of the Board of Directors required by the By-Laws or by law to be given and shall have such other powers and perform such other duties as may be prescribed by the Board of Directors or these By-Laws.
Treasurer. The Treasurer shall be the financial and accounting officer of the Corporation. The Treasurer shall monitor the receipt and payment of all the funds of the Corporation in the manner authorized by the Board of Directors. The Treasurer shall cause to be kept a book or books setting forth a true record of receipts and expenditures, assets and liabilities, losses and gains of the Corporation and shall send or cause to be given to the directors such financial statements and reports as required to be given by law, these By-Laws or by the Board of Directors.

shall not be valid unless each director is given at least seven (7) days written notice that the matter will be voted upon at a Board of Directors meeting, unless notice is waived in writing, signed by the director so waiving notice, and filed with the Corporation's minutes.

sections.title: Article VI. Corporate Records and Reports
sections.content:
Records. The Corporation shall maintain adequate and correct accounts, books and records of its business and properties and minutes of the proceedings of its Board of Directors. All of such books, records and accounts shall be kept at its principal place of business in the State of Pennsylvania as fixed by the Board of Directors from time to time.
Signing of Checks. All checks, drafts or other orders for payment of money, notes, or other indebtedness, issued in the name of or payable to the Corporation shall be signed or endorsed by such person or persons and in such manner as shall be determined from time to time by resolution of the Board of Directors.
Execution of Contracts. The Board of Directors may authorize any one or more officers, employees or agents to enter any contract or execute any instrument on behalf of the Corporation. Unless so authorized, no officer, agent or employee shall have any power or authority to bind the Corporation by any contract or engagement or to pledge its credit or to render it liable for any purpose or to any amount.
Annual Reports. The Board of Directors shall cause an annual report to be delivered to the Director of the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs for filing as required by law.
Article VII. Indemnification of Directors and Officers
Indemnification. To the fullest extent permitted by Pennsylvania law, the Corporation shall indemnify each director, officer and employee of the Corporation who is or was a party or is threatened to be made a party to any threatened, pending or completed action, suit or proceeding, whether civil, criminal, administrative or investigative and whether formal or informal, because such person is or was a director, officer or employee of the Corporation, or
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530 Page Number: 71
Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM
EHB Application Number: 242645 Grant Number: N/A Docusign Envelope ID: DA986F84-D034-4883-85C0-FFDA2F3166FF
is or was serving at the request of the Corporation as a director, officer or employee of another corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust or other enterprise, against all expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees), judgments, fines and amounts paid in settlement actually and reasonably incurred by him or her in connection with such action, suit or proceeding.
Advances for Expenses. The Corporation shall advance funds to pay for or reimburse the reasonable expenses and attorneys' fees incurred by a director, officer or employee before final disposition of an action, suit or proceeding, provided that such person delivers a written affirmation of such person's good faith belief that such person has met the requisite standard of conduct for indemnification and delivers a written undertaking to repay such amount if it is ultimately determined that such person did not meet the standard of conduct.
Continuing Indemnification. The indemnification shall be cumulative and not exclusive, and shall be in addition to any other indemnification provided by law or by any other agreement. Any repeal, amendment or modification of this Article shall not affect the indemnification provided in this Article for any acts or omissions occurring prior to such repeal, amendment or modification. The indemnification provided for in this Article shall continue as to any person who has ceased to be a director, officer or employee of the Corporation and shall inure to the benefit of such person's heirs, personal representatives, executors and administrators.
Insurance. The Corporation shall have the power to purchase and maintain insurance on behalf of any person who is or was a director, officer or employee of the Corporation, or is or was serving at the request of the Corporation as a director, officer or employee of another corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust or other enterprise against any liability asserted against him and incurred by him in any such capacity or arising out of his status as such, whether or not the Corporation would have the power to indemnify him against such liability under the provisions of this Article VII.

sections.title: Article VIII. Amendment to By-Laws
sections.content:
Amendment by Majority Vote. The By-Laws of this Corporation may from time to time be repealed, amended or altered, or new By-Laws may be adopted by the Board of Directors.
Limitation on Amendment. Anything herein to the contrary notwithstanding, these By- Laws shall not be amended in any manner which: alters the basic purpose of the Corporation as set forth herein and in the Articles of Incorporation, conflicts with any applicable law or government regulation; causes the diversion of any part of the Corporation for the purposes other than those authorized herein, deprives any eligible beneficiary of benefits; or causes the Corporation to lose its tax exempt status under State and Federal laws.

formFields.field: Laws shall not be amended in any manner which
formFields.value: alters the basic purpose of the Corporation

sections.title: Article IX. Dispute Resolution
sections.content:
Any and all disputes between or among any parties to these By-Laws or anyone claiming rights by, through or under any parties to these By-Laws that arise from or in any way relate to these By-Laws or any of their subject matter will be resolved by mediation pursuant to
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530 Page Number: 72 Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM

Docusign Envelope ID: DA986F84-D034-4883-85C0-FFDA2F3166FF
then applicable Mediation Rules, Procedures and Protocols of Dispute Prevention and Resolution, Inc. or its successor ("DPR"), and if not resolved by that mediation, then will be determined by final and binding arbitration pursuant to then applicable Arbitration Rules, Procedures and Protocols of DPR. The arbitrator will have the authority and discretion to determine and award legal and equitable relief, attorneys' fees and costs and arbitration fees and costs as the arbitrator deems fair and just. The arbitration award will be final and binding and will be enforceable as and by a court with jurisdiction over the parties.

sections.title: Article X. Legal Standing
sections.content: If any part of these By-Laws is illegal or invalid, such illegality or invalidity shall not affect the remaining parts of these By-Laws, unless such illegality or invalidity prevents the Board of Directors from accomplishing the purposes of the Corporation. If any illegality or invalidity exists, the Board of Directors shall immediately make the necessary amendments to eliminate the illegality or invalidity, and shall promulgate a new legal provision as similar as possible to the illegal or invalid provision, keeping the intent, as close as possible, of the illegal or invalid provision.

sections.title: Article XI: General Provisions
sections.content:
Patient Rights The Board shall respect patient confidentiality, patient rights, and will comply with health center policies
Fiscal Year. The fiscal year of the health center shall end on June 30 of each year.

sections.title: Preservation of Confidential Information.
sections.content:
The Board has adopted policies and will comply with all federal and state laws and regulations regarding the protection of confidential, privileged or proprietary information and all such provisions shall apply to all Members both during committee service and thereafter.

sections.title: CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER
sections.content:
The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) upholds the mission, values and principles of the organization and provides evidenced- based strategic vision, leadership, direction and oversight of all aspects of Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN). This includes organizational operations to manage financial health, employee well-being, quality services, compliance, brand identity, compliance, and overall organizational strategy. The CEO serves as the face of the organization with internal and external stakeholders and provides communication between board members and FPCSN executives. The CEO is directly employed by the health center and reports directly to the governing Board of Directors. ESSENTIAL DUTIES and RESPONSIBILITIES STRATEGIC LEADERSHIP
· Builds, leads, and manages the Executive Team, delegating authority and ensuring effective collaboration to cultivate and maintain high performing Executive and Leadership Teams.
· In collaboration with the Executive Team, provides visionary, strategic and innovative leadership ensuring alignment with FPCSN vision, mission, and long-term goals, and strategies.
· Leads, fosters and ensures accountability for a value-driven, equitable work environment for staff and patients. OPERATIONAL OVERSIGHT
· Collaborates with the Chief Operations Officer to ensure operational efficiency and effectiveness, ensuring alignment with the overall strategy of FPCSN.
· Collaborates with the Chief Clinical Officer to ensure high quality of care, fiscally responsible services and a high degree of patient satisfaction.
· Collaborates with the Executives, Senior Leaders and Board of Directors to set the course for health center expansion to new sites and new services/programs in response to community needs.
FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT
. Collaborates with the Chief Financial Officer to ensure sound fiscal operations and long-term sustainability.
· Oversight for the budget, financial planning, and resource allocation of FPCSN to ensure profitability and growth, grants and reporting are completed accurately and in a timely manner.
BOARD GOVERNANCE & STAKEHOLDER RELATIONS
· Engages with and informs the Board on Directors of FPCSNs operations and performance.
· Maintains relationships with the board of directors, funders, key partners, and other stakeholders.
· Fosters and maintains community partnerships and collaborations to provide services responsive to the community and staff.
PUBLIC REPRESENTATION
· Acts as the primary public face of FPCSN for media, public events, and other community relations.
· Represents FPCSN at the local, state and national levels and serves as the primary spokesperson for FPCSN.
· Participates in advocacy efforts for community health centers
POLICY AND COMPLIANCE
. Collaborates with the Chief Compliance Officer, ensures and maintains compliance with all federal, state and local regulatory standards.
. Collaborates with the Chief Human Resources Officer to ensure adherence to FPCSNs policies and procedures and a high degree of employee engagement.

sections.title: QUALITY & HEALTH INFORMATION
sections.content:
· Collaborates with the Chief Information Officer to ensure a robust, technically-sound, compliant information system and strategy in support of the business initiatives relative to security and maintenance of the technology network. • Serves on the CQI Committee, assuring that there is an annual plan approved by the Board of Directors.
QUALIFICATIONS
· Minimum of a Master's Degree in Public Health or Management field
· Minimum of three to five years in senior management in a non-profit, community organization
· Exhibit behaviors that align with FPCSN's organizational values, mission, and beliefs
· Demonstrate cultural competence/proficiency in interactions with others by treating co-workers, colleagues and those receiving service with respect and fairness at all times
· Ability to build and maintain positive and professional relationships based on respect, trust, and safety
· Demonstrate exemplary problem-solving, communication, interpersonal, and conflict resolution skills.
· Ability to work effectively as a team member
· High level of integrity. Highly responsible and reliable
· Exemplary organizational skills and ability to prioritize
· Commitment to service excellence for underrepresented and underserved populations
FPCSN Job Descriptions are reviewed annually and updated as often as necessary.

sections.title: CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER
sections.content: The Chief Operating Officer (COO) upholds the mission of FPCSN by ensuring efficient operations as a whole, integrating the strategic goals of the organization with the operational systems, while maximizing the income of the system. The COO coordinates, supervises, and manages the development and implementation of efficient administrative systems and general operations across FPCSN. The COO supports clinical operations by collaborating with the Chief Clinical Officer and Clinical Directors.

sections.title: Operational Leadership
sections.content:
· Supports the needs of all departments/health centers in the pursuit of excellence in care for patients.
· Facilitates collaborative decision-making processes that integrate all departments of FPCSN.
· Coordinates and ensures adherence to quality standards across FPCSN to ensure the delivery of high-quality services across all departments.
· Assists in the development and implementation of FPCSN policies and procedures.
· Supervise (including reflective supervision) and support the Center Directors, Facilities, Transportation, and Safety.
· Develop and implement a robust community engagement strategy for FPCSN centered around patient resources, in collaboration with the Center Directors and site-level outreach/community staff.
· Lead and support FPCSNs Shared Services Department and operations to ensure optimal systems and processes are in place.
· Support and promote interdepartmental communication and collaboration on FPCSN initiatives and teambuilding.
· Coordinates with the Chief Clinical Officer and Department Directors on clinical program operations.
· Assist in the development of Memorandums of Understanding (MOU) for FPCSN.
· Coordinates with the CEO and CHRO on staff engagement and retention.
· Assists in FPCSN growth, long-term planning and sustainability processes to carry forth FPCSN's mission into the future.
· Coordinates with the Fiscal team on financial operations matters.
· Support all departments in review of department level budget management to ensure financial stability for the organization.
· Support patient grievances in collaboration with Compliance.
· Coordinates and monitors productivity expectations across FPCSN and assists with developing and implementing actions plans, as necessary with department leads and practice managers.
· Assures the best contracts and prices for leased and purchased equipment.
· Maintains exemplary customer service throughout FPCSN and coordinates trainings as needed.
· Attends and participates in/on community/county committees and advisory boards.
· Maintains appropriate relationships with landlords or regulatory agencies of designated sites; as requested by supervisor.

sections.title: QUALIFICATIONS
sections.content:
· Master's Degree in Nursing, Business or Public Administration, Public Health or a related field
· Knowledge of federally qualified health centers/community health centers operations preferred
· Five years of management experience in a healthcare related industry preferred
· Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
· Exhibit behaviors that align with FPCN's organizational values, mission, and beliefs
· Demonstrate cultural competence/proficiency in interactions with others by treating co-workers, colleagues and those receiving service with respect and fairness at all times and assist in fostering a culture that promotes racial equity
· Ability to build and maintain positive and professional relationships based on respect, trust, and safety.
· Demonstrate exemplary problem-solving, communication, interpersonal, and conflict resolution skills.
· Ability to work effectively as a team member
· Ability to seek feedback and encourage collaborative input in decisions
· Highly responsible and reliable as well as courteous
· Exemplary organizational skills and ability to prioritize
· High skill in operating personal computer utilizing word processing, spreadsheets, databases and e-mail
· Ability to perform public relations and public speaking in a professional manner
· Ability to maintain confidentiality
· Ability to competently supervise, coach and develop staff with varied educational and experiential background
FPCSN Job Descriptions are reviewed annually and updated as often as necessary.

sections.title: CHIEF CLINICAL OFFICER
sections.content: The Chief Clinical Officer upholds the mission and values of FPCSN by overseeing all Clinical Departments and assuring the delivery of all clinical care is high quality, evidence-based, satisfying to patients and staff, and cost-effective; promotes a fiscally sound organization. The Chief Clinical Officer serves as a member of the Executive Team, participating in network-wide strategy development, planning, and decision-making.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL DUTIES and RESPONSIBILITIES
sections.content:
· In collaboration with the CEO and Chief Compliance Officer, ensure compliance with all Federal/HRSA, State, Local and other grant requirements.
· Maintains current knowledge regarding today's healthcare environment, including all FPCSN clinical disciplines
· Collaborates with the Clinical Directors, develops, implements and evaluates all clinical policies and procedures ensuring that the Clinical Departments are operating effectively and efficiently.
· Works collaboratively with the Director of Finance to ensure fiscally sound practices within the FPCSN Clinical Departments.
· Works collaboratively with Executive Team to ensure effective development, implementation and communication of FPCSNs policies and procedures.
· Supervision of all FPCSN Clinical Directors.
· Represents FPCSN to outside agencies and entities in the Executive Clinician role.
· Coordinates monthly meetings with Clinical Department leadership.
· Coordinates QI efforts for clinical departments, set network goals and promote clinical departmental accountability.
· Works collaboratively with the Quality Manager, P4P Manager and Clinical Directors on all required clinical data reporting.
· Values, understands and ensures each of the clinical departments of FPCSN (clinical care, operations, staffing model, budget/fiscal, etc.) provides high quality, evidence-based care.
· Increase integration among clinical disciplines in collaboration with all Clinical Directors.
· Represents the clinical voice of all disciplines in decision making (grants, programs, operations, staffing, budget/fiscal, etc.)
· In partnership with all Clinical Departments, promotes and advocates for clinicians, clinical staff, and clinical practice internally to support healthcare professionals.
· Represents/advocates FPCSN clinical professions to external partners or governing bodies to advance and influence clinical practice at the local, regional, and state levels.
· Clinically practices at least 1 day/week.

sections.title: QUALIFICATIONS
sections.content:
· Current Pennsylvania Physician or Nurse Practitioner license
· At least 5 years of practicing as a licensed Physician or Nurse Practitioner
· At least 5 years of leadership experience working with an integrated healthcare facility with multiple clinical disciplines, specifically Dental, Primary Care, Behavioral Health
· At least five years' experience in healthcare management
· Experience with pediatrics, adults and managing chronic conditions is a must
· Experience working in Behavioral Health is highly preferred
· Leadership and management skills necessary to work effectively within a system, influence results, motivate individuals and teams, and resolve problems in a diplomatic, professional manner.
· Excellent relationship, oral and written communication, persuasion, negotiation, and presentation skills. The candidate must be able to clearly articulate factual and advisory information to all levels of staff, executives, the boards of directors, and others, and garner related support.
· Computer skills, including proficiency in Microsoft Office. A personal presence that is characterized by a sense of honesty, integrity, and caring with the ability to inspire and motivate others to promote the philosophy, mission, vision, goals, and values of FPCSN.
FPCSN Job Descriptions are reviewed annually and updated as often as necessary.

sections.title: CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER
sections.content: The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) is a key member of the executive leadership team responsible for the financial strategy, stewardship, and sustainability of the organization. The CFO provides leadership in all financial operations, including revenue cycle management, budgeting, accounting, grants management, and capital planning. The CFO must possess extensive experience in value-based care models, a deep understanding of FQHC regulations and guidelines (including HRSA, 45 CFR Part 75, and UDS reporting), and proven expertise in managing the unique financial dynamics of a multi-site healthcare organization.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL DUTIES and RESPONSIBILITIES Strategic Financial Leadership
sections.content:
· Develop and implement financial strategies to support the FQHC's mission and growth, including initiatives tied to value-based reimbursement models.
· Advise the CEO and Board of Directors on financial planning, risk management, and long-term sustainability.
· Lead development of annual operating and capital budgets, ensuring alignment with organizational priorities and federal grant requirements.
Financial Operations
· Oversee all accounting, financial reporting, and internal controls in compliance with GAAP and FQHC guidelines.
· Ensure timely and accurate preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements, including HRSA UDS cost reporting and grant-specific reports.
· Lead the development and execution of comprehensive organizational budgets, aligning financial planning with strategic initiatives, growth objectives, and sustainability goals while ensuring resources are allocated to drive mission-critical programs and value-based care priorities.
· Provide strategic oversight and direction for the revenue cycle, including billing, coding, and collections, to optimize reimbursement, strengthen cash flow, and ensure alignment with the organization's long-term financial and operational goals.
Value-Based Care & Managed Care
· Lead financial modeling and analytics to support participation in value-based care initiatives (e.g., ACOs, PCMH, quality incentive programs, shared savings).
· Monitor and analyze performance metrics under pay-for-performance contracts, driving initiatives that improve quality scores and financial outcomes.
Clinical Operations & Sustainability
· Understand FQHC clinical operations and how patient flow, provider productivity, service mix, and operational capacity directly impact the organization's overall financial sustainability.
Grants & Compliance
· Manage budgeting and reporting for federal, state, and private grants (e.g., Section 330 funding, Ryan White, 340B program).
· Ensure compliance with HRSA Health Center Program Requirements, Uniform Guidance (45 CFR Part 75), and other federal and state regulations.
Team Leadership
· Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing finance team; foster collaboration with operations, clinical, and quality teams.
· Serve as the primary financial liaison to the Board's Finance Committee, preparing detailed reports and recommendations.

sections.title: QUALIFICATIONS
sections.content:
. Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; CPA or MBA strongly preferred.
· 7-10 years of progressive financial leadership experience, preferably in a healthcare or FQHC setting.
· Demonstrated expertise in value-based care financial models (shared savings, quality incentives, risk-based contracts).
· In-depth knowledge of FQHC operations, HRSA guidelines, UDS reporting, and 340B program.
· Strong understanding of revenue cycle management, cost reporting, and healthcare reimbursement methodologies.
· Experience managing federal and state grant funding, including budgeting, drawdowns, and reporting.
· Strategic thinker with the ability to align financial management with organizational mission.
· Exceptional analytical skills and ability to present complex data to non-financial audiences.
· Collaborative leadership style; proven ability to lead cross-functional teams.
· High integrity, transparency, and commitment to compliance.
· Prior leadership experience within a Federally Qualified Health Center or similar community health setting.
· Knowledge of accounting software (NetSuite) and familiarity with EHR/PM systems (Athena) to integrate clinical and financial data.
· Experience navigating HRSA Operational Site Visit (OSV) requirements from a finance perspective.
FPCSN Job Descriptions are reviewed annually and updated as often as necessary.

sections.title: CHIEF INFORMATION OFFICER
sections.content: The CIO leads the organization in planning and implementing enterprise technology systems to support the mission and vision of FPCSN. This individual has responsibility for strategic planning, operational planning, security, compliance, and overall administration of enterprise information support systems. The CIO will lead a system of complex technology resources, a diverse team, and developing partnerships across the organization to enhance programmatic and administrative information systems. The CIO is also responsible for the activities related to the availability, integrity, and confidentiality of our patient, customer, employee, funder, and business information in compliance with applicable laws and the organization's information security policies. The CIO is responsible for establishing and maintaining a corporate-wide information security management program to ensure information technology assets are protected. In addition, this individual will partner with risk management, legal, compliance, and executive leadership to manage organizational risk associated with information systems technology.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL DUTIES and RESPONSIBILITIES
sections.content:
· Promote and support a working environment consistent with FPCSN Values
· Develop, lead, and support an integrated team responsible for managing the core technology infrastructure, including wireless, wired, data center, disaster recovery, cloud environment, information technology security, applications, and related project management.
· In partnership with leadership from Clinical Departments, Shared Services, and Programs inform, develop, and implement Information Technology Initiatives, based on market trends, changes to service delivery models, and other organizational needs, as part of the strategic and operational plans for FPCSN
· Develop and communicate strategic operational plans, including KPIs, specific goals, and success measures
. Develop, manage, and oversee the operating, personnel, and capital budgets for all IT and Informatics.
· Provide direction and leadership in the acquisition, application, and use of technology across the organization through a governance structure that aligns technology initiatives with business priorities while balancing resource constraints (human and fiscal) to optimize technology investments
· Partner with operational leadership on the ongoing maintenance and validation of a Business Continuity Plan.
· Provide leadership, direction, and support ensuring the IT and Informatics teams provides excellent customer service and satisfies the technology and supports requirements and needs of our staff, service lines, departments and programs.
· Ensure that enterprise information systems operate according to internal standards, external accrediting agency standards and legal requirements.
· Maintain and monitor a strategic, comprehensive enterprise information security and information technology risk management program; including a security management and reporting framework.
· Partner with risk management, legal, compliance, and executive leadership, to determine the acceptable level of organizational informational technology risk.
· Provide leadership, guidance, and support towards achieving the goals of the organization's procurement process and related initiatives.

sections.title: QUALIFICATIONS
sections.content:
· Minimum Bachelor's Degree in Information Systems, Engineering or related area from an accredited institution is required. A Master's Degree is preferred.
· Minimum of 5 - 10 years demonstrated experience with IT systems and IT management at a senior level, including executive level, with a sequence of increasing responsibilities in large and diverse business settings.
· Experience and/or familiarity with the health care industry
· A track record of successful large project implementations. Demonstrated project management skills.
· Demonstrated ability to deliver technology solutions that align to business needs in collaboration with leadership across the organization
· Demonstrated technical foundation including- but not limited to: SaaS implementations, Cybersecurity, Data Management and Business Analytics, Network Infrastructure, and Cloud Computing
· Excellent communication skills (written and oral) with the ability to engage and work collaboratively with diverse groups of people
· Excellent listening, relationship building, collaboration and conflict management skills
· Demonstrated skills in organizational leadership and management with the ability to coach and develop staff, create and lead high-performance teams, set and achieve strategic objectives, and manage to financial and high-quality service- delivery objectives.
· Demonstrated ability to translate technology for non-technical individuals
· Demonstrated success as a customer driven leader.
FPCSN Job Descriptions are reviewed annually and updated as often as necessary.

sections.title: WRITTEN REFERRAL ARRANGEMENTS University of Pennsylvania Health System
sections.content:
Type of Agreement: Transfer and Referral Agreement
Description: University of Pennsylvania Health System (UPHS) accepts referrals of patients for emergency, outpatient, and inpatient medical services regardless of ability to pay. These services are provided in the Hospital and clinics and are provided based on the hospital's Financial Assistance Policy that meets or exceeds the FPCSN sliding scale that is compliant with Health Center Program requirements. The agreement details the services provided, including prenatal, intrapartum, and postnatal care, as well as provision of information between FPCSN and UPHS and referral of patients back to FPCSN. Referrals are tracked until results and/or information about services are received, so FPCSN can reach out to patients and follow up with services.
Timeframe:
Beginning May 5, 2023 and Ongoing

DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH AMBULATORY HEALTH SERVICES 1101 Market Street, 11th Floor Philadelphia, Pennsylvania 19107 Tel: (215) 685-6769 Fax (215) 685-6732
Palak Raval-Nelson, PhD, MPH Health Commissioner
Cheryl Kramer-Sylvester, MBA Director - Ambulatory Health Services
September 16, 2025
Emily Nichols, MPH Chief Executive Officer Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. 4700 Wissahickon Avenue, Suite 118 Philadelphia, PA 19144
Dear Ms. Nichols,
On behalf of Philadelphia Department of Public Health (PDPH), I write in full support of the Service Area Competition application submitted by the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA). For more than 33 years, FPCSN has been a vital presence in Philadelphia's healthcare landscape, first as a Federally Qualified Health Center as part of Resources for Human Development, and now as a standalone non-profit Community Health Center. Throughout this legacy of providing care, FPCSN has consistently upheld the standards and requirements of the HRSA Health Center Program, providing essential care to communities across Philadelphia.
Recent analyses confirm the significant unmet need for primary care in West/Southwest and North Philadelphia, where the FPCSN service area is located. In 2023, the Federally Qualified Health Centers served just 38.7% of the population in these neighborhoods, leaving an estimated 298,000 individuals without access to care through a community health center. These communities face well-documented challenges, including higher rates of chronic illness, limited preventive and oral healthcare, and gaps in prenatal and behavioral health services.
PDPH operates nine FQHC/Look Alike heath centers that serve the entire City, and our experience affirms the magnitude of the need. The demand for high-quality, affordable, and accessible primary care far exceeds the current capacity. FPCSN's comprehensive model, which integrates primary care, behavioral health, oral health, and wellness services, strengthens the overall safety net and improves access for patients who might otherwise go without needed care.
PDPH and FPCSN have long collaborated through the Health Federation of Philadelphia and the Pennsylvania Association of Community Health Centers (PACHC). We share a mission to expand healthcare access, ensuring that care is delivered with dignity and respect. PDPH is committed to continuing this partnership, recognizing that collective effort is essential to meeting the health needs of our region.
For these reasons, I strongly support FPCSN's Service Area Competition application. I am confident that, with HRSA's continued support, FPCSN will sustain and expand its impact as an essential healthcare provider for Philadelphia and its surrounding communities.
Sincerely,
Cheryl Kramer-Sylvester Director, Ambulatory Health Services Cheryl Kramer-Sylvester

sections.content:
September 15, 2025
Emily Nichols, MPH Chief Executive Officer Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. 4700 Wissahickon Avenue, Suite 118 Philadelphia, PA 19144
Dear Ms. Nichols,
On behalf of Greater Philadelphia Health Action, Inc. (GPHA), I write in full support of the Service Area Competition application submitted by the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA). For more than 33 years, FPCSN has been a vital presence in Philadelphia's healthcare landscape, first as a Federally Qualified Health Center as part of Resources for Human Development, and now as a standalone non-profit Community Health Center. Throughout this legacy of providing care, FPCSN has consistently upheld the standards and requirements of the HRSA Health Center Program, providing essential care to communities across Philadelphia and nearby Delaware County.
Recent analyses confirm the significant unmet need for primary care in West/Southwest and North Philadelphia, where the FPCSN service area is located. In 2023, the Federally Qualified Health Centers served just 38.7% of the population in these neighborhoods, leaving an estimated 298,000 individuals without access to care through a community health center. These communities face well-documented challenges, including higher rates of chronic illness, limited preventive and oral healthcare, and gaps in prenatal and behavioral health services.
GPHA also serves portions of this region, and our experience affirms the magnitude of the need. The demand for high-quality, affordable, and accessible primary care far exceeds the current capacity. FPCSN's comprehensive model, which integrates primary care, behavioral health, oral health, and wellness services, strengthens the overall safety net and improves access for patients who might otherwise go without needed care.
GPHA and FPCSN have long collaborated through the Health Federation of Philadelphia and the Pennsylvania Association of Community Health Centers (PACHC). We share a mission to expand healthcare access, ensuring that care is delivered with dignity and respect. We are committed to continuing this partnership, recognizing that collective effort is essential to meeting the health needs of our region.
For these reasons, I strongly support FPCSN's Service Area Competition application. I am confident that, with HRSA's continued support, FPCSN will sustain and expand its impact as an essential healthcare provider for Philadelphia and its surrounding communities.
Sincerely,
Brian Clark CEO/Executive Director

Emily Nichols, MPH Chief Executive Officer Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. 4700 Wissahickon Avenue, Suite 118 Philadelphia, PA 19144
September 15, 2025
Dear Ms. Nichols,
On behalf of Project HOME, I express strong support for the Service Area Competition application submitted by the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA). For over 33 years, FPCSN has been a trusted provider of essential care in Philadelphia-first as part of Resources for Human Development, and now as a standalone non-profit Community Health Center. Throughout, it has consistently met HRSA's standards while serving communities across Philadelphia and Delaware County.
Recent data shows the urgent need for primary care in FPCSN's service area. In 2023, only 38.7% of residents in West/Southwest and North Philadelphia accessed care through Federally Qualified Health Centers, leaving nearly 298,000 without access. These neighborhoods face deep disparities in chronic disease, preventive care, oral health, and behavioral and prenatal services.
At Project HOME, we see this unmet need daily. FPCSN's integrated model-offering primary, behavioral, oral health, and wellness services-greatly expands the local safety net and reaches patients who might otherwise go without care.
Our longstanding partnership with FPCSN, through the Health Federation of Philadelphia and PACHC, reflects our shared mission to deliver healthcare with dignity. We look forward to continuing this work together.
For these reasons, I fully support FPCSN's application and urge HRSA to continue investing in this essential provider.
Sincerely,

Emily Nichols, MPH Chief Executive Officer Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. 4700 Wissahickon Avenue, Suite 118 Philadelphia, PA 19144
Dear Ms. Nichols,
On behalf of the Health Federation of Philadelphia (HFP), I write in full and unwavering support of the Service Area Competition application submitted by the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA). For more than 33 years, FPCSN has been a vital presence in Philadelphia's healthcare landscape, first as a Federally Qualified Health Center as part of Resources for Human Development and now as a standalone non-profit Community Health Center. Throughout this legacy of providing care, FPCSN has consistently upheld the standards and requirements of the HRSA Health Center Program, providing essential care to communities across Philadelphia and nearby Delaware County.
Recent analyses confirm the significant unmet need for primary care in West/Southwest and North Philadelphia, where the FPCSN service area is located. In 2023, Federally Qualified Health Centers served just 38.7% of the population in these neighborhoods, leaving an estimated 298,000 individuals without access to care through a community health center. These communities face well-documented challenges, including higher rates of chronic illness, limited preventive and oral healthcare, and gaps in prenatal and behavioral health services. FPCSN's comprehensive model, which integrates primary care, behavioral health, oral health, and wellness services, strengthens the overall safety net and improves access for patients who might otherwise go without needed care. Furthermore, FPCSN has been an innovator and early adopter of best practices, a trusted partner and a true champion of community health.
The Health Federation is a HRSA-funded HCCN, as well as a membership network of the FQHCs in Southeastern Pennsylvania. FPCSN has been a longstanding member of HFP and of the HCCN, and we value your engagement. The CEO of FPCSN is also a HFP Board Member and a member of the Board Executive Committee. HFP has a long history of working collaboratively with FPCSN and its other members in many areas, including health information technology, quality improvement, and projects to improve chronic and infectious disease management.
For these reasons, I strongly support FPCSN's Service Area Competition application. I am confident that, with HRSA's continued support, FPCSN will sustain and expand its impact as an essential healthcare provider for Philadelphia and its surrounding communities.
Sincerely,
Natalie Levkovich
Chief Executive Officer

sections.title: Penn Medicine University of Pennsylvania Health System
sections.content:
Kevin B. Mahoney Chief Executive Officer
October 1, 2025
Bureau of Primary Health Care Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA) U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
To Health Center Program Officers,
On behalf of Penn Medicine, I am writing to express my strong support for the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) in their application to the Health Resources and Services Administration's (HRSA) Service Area Competition (SAC) (HRSA 26-004). For more than 33 years, FPCSN has been a cornerstone in Philadelphia's healthcare landscape, first as a Federally Qualified Health Center as part of Resources for Human Development, and now as a standalone nonprofit community health center. Throughout this legacy, FPCSN has consistently exceeded standards and requirements of the HRSA Health Center Program, delivering high-quality, comprehensive care to communities across Philadelphia and Delaware County.
Penn Medicine shares FPCSN's unwavering commitment to advancing access to high-quality health care for all. FPCSN has demonstrated a long and successful history of providing patient centered, affordable care to medical underserved populations. Their integrated model, which combines primary care, behavioral health, and supportive services, reflects best practices for improving health outcomes, addressing the social determinants of health, and reducing health disparities.
Our organizations have collaborated closely to coordinate care for patients with complex medical and social needs, reduce avoidable emergency department utilization, and strengthen linkages between hospital-based and community-based care. Through shared clinical programming, such as community-based prenatal care and integrated behavioral healthcare, we have seen firsthand the impact of FPCSN's patient-centered model of care.
FPCSN represents the best of what community health centers offer - trusted, efficient, and compassionate care that empowers patients and strengthens neighborhoods. HRSA support will enable FPCSN to expand and sustain their essential services, build organizational capacity, and ensure that even more individuals and families can access comprehensive and affordable care, close to home.
Thank you for your consideration and for your ongoing commitment to supporting organizations that serve the most vulnerable members of our communities
Sincerely,
Kevin B. Mahony
Kevin B. Mahoney Chief Executive Officer
Perelman Center for Advanced Medicine 3400 Civic Center Boulevard, A-5 | Philadelphia, PA 19104-4283 | 215.662.2203 | kevin.mahoney@pennmedicine.upenn.edu

Emily Nichols, MPH Chief Executive Officer Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. 4700 Wissahickon Avenue, Suite 118 Philadelphia, PA 19144
Dear Ms. Nichols,
On behalf of the Philadelphia Housing Authority (PHA), I write in full support of the Service Area Competition application submitted by the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA). For more than 33 years, FPCSN has been a vital presence in Philadelphia's healthcare landscape, first as a Federally Qualified Health Center as part of Resources for Human Development, and now as a standalone non-profit Community Health Center. Throughout this legacy of providing care, FPCSN has consistently upheld the standards and requirements of the HRSA Health Center Program, providing essential care to communities across Philadelphia. The FPCSN service sites are accessible to many of the PHA public housing and the service area includes West/Southwest Philadelphia, an area the City has found to be without adequate access to primary health care.
PHA is concerned about the health problems experienced by the public housing and low-income communities that FPCSN serves. FPCSN services have long been targeted to residents of public housing and the surrounding communities, which face well-documented challenges, including higher rates of chronic illness, limited preventive and oral healthcare, and gaps in prenatal and behavioral health services. The area FQHCs serve just 38.7% of the population in these neighborhoods, leaving an estimated 298,000 individuals without access to care through a community health center.
The demand for high-quality, affordable, and accessible primary care far exceeds the current capacity in Philadelphia. FPCSN's comprehensive model, which integrates primary care, behavioral health, oral health, and wellness services, strengthens the overall safety net and improves access for patients who might otherwise go without needed care. PHA depends upon FPCSN's expertise and has a long collaborative relationship with your health centers. FPCSN sites are noted on the PHA website among the trusted resources there.
For these reasons, I strongly support FPCSN's Service Area Competition application. I am confident that, with HRSA's continued support, FPCSN will sustain and expand its impact as an essential healthcare provider for Philadelphia and its surrounding communities.
Sincerely,
Kelvin A. Jeremiah, President & CEO Philadelphia Housing Authority

October 7, 2025
Emily Nichols, MPH Chief Executive Officer Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. 4700 Wissahickon Avenue, Suite 118 Philadelphia, PA 19144
Dear Ms. Nichols:
The Pennsylvania Department of Human Services (PA DHS) is submitting this letter of support regarding Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. Service Are Competition Application to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA). PA DHS supports the supply and distribution of primary care practitioners, particularly in underserved areas, that align with the Pennsylvania Medical Assistance (MA, also known as Medicaid), the HealthChoices mandatory MA managed care program and the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP). FPCSN has a history of supporting the Philadelphia area as a Federally Qualified Health Care Center (FQHC) and seeks to achieve that status again.
The PA DHS Office of Medical Assistance Programs continually assesses and seeks to assure both a sufficient supply of primary care practitioners and an appropriate level of access to primary care for all MA and CHIP beneficiaries and works to accomplish those aims through a number of program areas. One of the important elements in the overall approach to providing health care to underserved populations is the role of the community health centers. These centers are specifically tasked to provide health care to individuals who receive their coverage through MA. PA DHS supports the mission and goals of the community health centers and their goals of providing health care services, primary care, behavioral health care and oral health to those who need it in their communities.
We look forward to collaborating and continuing to work with FPCSN as a community health center and again as a FQHC in the Philadelphia area to improve the health of Pennsylvania's MA and CHIP beneficiaries.
Sincerely,
Saucy a. Negen
Sally A. Kozak, MHA, RN
Deputy Secretary

Thomas J. Engels Administrator Health Resources and Services Administration U.S. Department of Health and Human Services 5600 Fishers Lane Rockville, MD 20857 Re: Family Practice & Counseling Services Network Service Area Competition Application
October 6, 2025
Dear Administrator Engels:
As the U.S. Representative for Pennsylvania's 5th Congressional District, which includes large portions of South and Southwest Philadelphia, I am pleased to offer my strong support for the Service Area Competition application submitted by the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA).
For more than three decades, FPCSN has been a trusted partner in advancing community health across the city of Philadelphia. Since I was first elected in 2018, my office has worked alongside FPCSN in advocating for access to affordable, high-quality care that the residents of Philadelphia and others served by FPCSN deserve. Our shared commitment to increasing access to care and improving health outcomes has helped bring crucial federal resources into neighborhoods with the highest needs - where too many individuals and families would otherwise go without timely primary, behavioral, and oral healthcare.
The challenges in our mutual service area within Philadelphia are well-documented: high rates of chronic disease, gaps in prenatal and preventive services, and persistent barriers to access. Federally Qualified Health Centers in the area recently served just 40% of the population in these neighborhoods, leaving nearly 400,000 low-income people unserved, including more than 61,000 uninsured residents. The Service Area Competition represents an opportunity for FPCSN to continue its vital work and ensure Philadelphia residents have proper access to quality healthcare - regardless of their income, zip code, or insurance status.

This shared commitment has helped bring federal resources into neighborhoods where the need is greatest, and where too many residents would otherwise go without timely primary, behavioral, or oral healthcare.

Area Federally Qualified Health Centers recently served just 40% of the population in these neighborhoods, leaving an estimated 395,214 low-income people unserved, including 61,812 uninsured residents. sections.title: United States Senate WASHINGTON, DC 20510 October 03, 2025
sections.content:
Thomas Engels Health Resources and Services Administration U.S. Department of Health and Human Services 5600 Fishers Lane Rockville, MD 20857
Dear Mr. Engels,
I write in support of Family Practice & Counseling Services Network's (FPCSN) application for the Service Area Competition grant. This funding would support FPCN's dedication to providing care in their community through primary, behavioral, and oral health services.
FPCSN is a network of federally qualified community health centers that use a nurse-managed, integrated model to deliver coordinated care for the entirety of the patient. For more than three decades, FPCSN has stood as a trusted partner in advancing community health across Philadelphia and continues to advocate for access to affordable, high-quality care.
There is a well-documented need for this type of comprehensive, coordinated care that FPCSN provides; the communities they serve face disproportionately high rates of chronic disease, significant gaps in prenatal and preventive services, and ongoing barriers to accessing essential healthcare.
With this grant funding, FPCSN can also strengthen outreach and care coordination, ensuring that uninsured and underinsured patients remain connected to continuous, comprehensive services rather than falling through the cracks of the healthcare system. Additionally, the funding would enable FPCSN to provide comprehensive care to uninsured patients who would otherwise go without critical primary, behavioral, and oral health services.
I urge you to give the application full and fair consideration. Feel free to contact my office (202) 224-4254 if you have any questions, comments, or concerns. Thank you in advance for your consideration.
Sincerely,
John Fetterman United States Senator

children.content:
WWW.REPMCCLINTON.COM
I am writing to express my strong support for the Service Area Competition application submitted by the Family Practice & Counseling Services Network (FPCSN) to the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA).
FPCSN has been a fixture in our region for over three decades, growing to several comprehensive health centers in Philadelphia, including the Health Annex in Southwest Philadelphia that I am proud to represent in the Pennsylvania House of Representatives. As you know, my office has partnered with, and worked alongside FPCSN for years, advocating for access to affordable, high- quality care that citizens of Philadelphia and beyond deserve. This shared commitment has helped bring resources into neighborhoods where need is the greatest, and where too many residents would otherwise go without timely primary, behavioral, or oral healthcare. As we continue to look for ways to increase access and affordability across the board, I would like to thank FPCSN for the vital work your organization has done in Philadelphia on these fronts.
The health challenges in our area are well-documented - high rates of chronic disease, gaps in prenatal and preventive services, and persistent barriers to care. The General Assembly is working hard to address each of these in our area and across Pennsylvania, but we cannot do it alone. We need partners such as FPCSN to help our residents get the care they expect and deserve. Despite our shared efforts, gaps persist.
Consider that area Federally Qualified Health Centers recently served just 40% of the population in these vulnerable neighborhoods, leaving an estimated 395,214 low-income people unserved, including 61,812 uninsured residents. The Service Area Competition represents not only the continuation of FPCSN's vital work but also the fulfillment of the vision we have collectively championed, that quality healthcare is a right, not a privilege. In 2025, healthcare access should not be dependent on an individual's zip code.
Tracking Number: GRANT14484530 Page Number: 121 Funding Opportunity Number: HRSA-26-004 Received Date: 10/24/2025 5:10:43 PM
EHB Application Number: 242645
Grant Number: N/A
Page 2
FPCSN's integrated model of care, including its school-based health centers and community partnerships, reflects the kind of innovation and collaboration that strengthens our city's safety net, at a time when many in our community are dealing with a number of issues including access to health services and food insecurity. Your organization's mission directly aligns with our role as civic leaders: to improve the wellbeing of the people we represent, to ensure accessible healthcare services, and to advocate for sustainable systems of care. While these challenges still remain, it is clearly evident that FPCSN's work is more vital and more necessary than ever before.
For these reasons and many others, I fully support FPCSN's Service Area Competition application and would like to reaffirm my commitment to continued collaboration in advancing wellness for all in Philadelphia, our region, and commonwealth.
Sincerely,
Joanna & mache
Joanna E. McClinton Speaker of the House 191st Legislative District

Dear Applicant:
We're pleased to tell you we determined you're exempt from federal income tax under Internal Revenue Code (IRC) Section 501(c)(3). Donors can deduct contributions they make to you under IRC Section 170. You're also qualified to receive tax deductible bequests, devises, transfers or gifts under Section 2055, 2106, or 2522. This letter could help resolve questions on your exempt status. Please keep it for your records.
Organizations exempt under IRC Section 501(c)(3) are further classified as either public charities or private foundations. We determined you're a public charity under the IRC Section listed at the top of this letter.
If we indicated at the top of this letter that you're required to file Form 990/990-EZ/990-N, our records show you're required to file an annual information return (Form 990 or Form 990-EZ) or electronic notice (Form 990-N, the e-Postcard). If you don't file a required return or notice for three consecutive years, your exempt status will be automatically revoked.
If we indicated at the top of this letter that an addendum applies, the enclosed addendum is an integral part of this letter.
For important information about your responsibilities as a tax-exempt organization, go to www.irs.gov/charities. Enter "4221-PC" in the search bar to view Publication 4221-PC, Compliance Guide for 501(c)(3) Public Charities, which describes your recordkeeping, reporting, and disclosure requirements.
Sincerely, stephen a. martin

sections.title: Family Practice & Counseling Services Network 2026 Service Area Competition Application
SLIDING FEE DISCOUNT PROGRAM
sections.content:
Sliding Fee Applicability, Definitions, and Alignment with FPG
FPCSN policy, approved by the Board, is that all required primary, preventive, dental, behavioral health, and additional health services are available and accessible to the target population without regard to patients' ability to pay, and that the health center has a sliding fee discount scale geared to U.S. poverty guidelines. It is the policy of FPCSN to uniformly apply fee discounts to all patients deemed eligible, based on the most recent Federal Poverty Guidelines.
The FPCSN sliding scale is based solely on income and family size and is reviewed periodically regarding affordability. The definitions used in the policy for income and family are those of the US Census. Income is defined as all cash receipts before taxes with certain adjustments and does not include non-cash benefits such as SNAP, school lunch programs, clothing vouchers, or food/rent in lieu of wages. Family is defined as a group of two or more persons related by birth, marriage, or adoption who live together; students, regardless of residence, who are supported by their parents are considered to be living in same household. An individual living alone or in a household with individuals outside of their family is defined as a family of one.
Sliding Fee Assessment
FPCSN policy requires that patients are made aware of the sliding fee discount program by providing information on the health center website, posting signs in each site, and notifying patients during appointment scheduling and registration. Patients are asked annually to provide their family size and income information for an assessment of their eligibility for discounted fees. Patients applying for the sliding fee scale discount must present income documentation to determine income level that includes: previous year's tax return, current pay stub, pension check stub, beneficiary award letter (Social Security benefits, etc.), or personal attestation of earnings for persons with no tax liability and/or who are self-employed. If patients do not have proper documentation with them, they are provided time to provide it for the discount to be applied to the visit. Once a discount has been approved, it is honored for a year, at which time the patient must reapply. Patients have the right to refuse to complete the assessment; they will be ineligible for discounts.
Sliding Fee Schedule Structure and Nominal Fees
FPCN has sliding fee scales for Primary Care, Behavioral Health, Integrated Behavioral Health, and Dental services. The FPCSN sliding scales are revised annually and approved by the Board, and include three categories between 100% and 200% of federal poverty level, includes definitions of family size, and is used for all clinical services. (See sliding scale in Attachment 10) The scales and procedures are on hand for staff who register patients and they receive training in how charges are adjusted according to health center policy. The scales are embedded within the EHR to automatically calculate the discounts once family size and income are entered.
Charges for patients with income below 100% of poverty are set at nominal levels that can be waived in some cases. The nominal charges are flat charges, are not set at the actual cost of the service provided, and are set at levels designed to be nominal from the perspective of the patient.
