sections.title: Family Practice & Counseling Services Network 2026 Service Area Competition Application
sections.content: The sliding scales go up to 200% of federal poverty level for most services and 250% for family planning services covered by a state program, and the scales are used for all clinical services. There is no charge for lab work for uninsured patients and it is included as a part of the primary care visit. Attempts are made to collect the discounted fees, but patients are not refused care as a result of non-payment. Hence, no one is denied services due to their inability to pay.

sections.title: KEY MANAGEMENT STAFF
sections.content: As noted in Form 2, the organization chart, the position descriptions, and the key management resumes attached, all of the managers, including the Executive Director who is the CEO, are hired directly by FPCSN. The Executive Director reports directly to the Board and the Board has the authority and responsibility for the selection/dismissal and evaluation of that position. The Executive Director is responsible to select and dismiss all personnel working in the health center and to assure there is a process for filling vacant key management positions. The Executive Director supervises the other members of the executive team and assures that Board policies are carried out.

sections.title: CONTRACTS AND SUBAWARDS
sections.content:
As noted in the narrative, Attachment 7, and Form 8, Family Practice & Counseling Services Network is a private 501(c)3 organization registered in Pennsylvania and with the IRS. FPCSN does not have a parent, affiliate, or subsidiary organization. It has no contracts with any other organization to perform substantive programmatic work within the proposed scope of project, and no subawards to carry out any portion of the project.
FPCSN financial accounting and internal control systems are designed to ensure that all federal funds are fully accounted for, including maintaining source documentation, that all expenditures of federal award funds are allowable, and that only actual work performed is charged to any award. Federal funds received will be tracked in a separate general ledger account. Salaries and wages supported by federal funds will be recorded in these separate general ledger accounts, while other non-personnel costs are reported in a separate cost center.

sections.title: COLLABORATIVE RELATIONSHIPS
sections.content: As noted in the narrative and Attachment 9, FPCSN coordinates and integrates its activities with other health services in the service area, consistent with those noted in Attachment 1. The health care community in the Philadelphia area solidly supports the new entity operating the health center.

sections.title: BILLING AND COLLECTIONS
sections.content:
a. Participation in Medicare, Medicaid, Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP) As noted in Form 3, FPCSN participates in Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP, and other insurers. It has contracts with all MCOs in the service area. FPCSN's eligibility determination process allows patients to have their insurance eligibility assessed prior to receiving services. The eligibility verification is completed via an Internet capability utilizing a combination of AthenaPractice eligibility, Promise (State Medicaid) Eligibility, and Navinet eligibility verification portals. The billing process involves claims being submitted to the payors utilizing the ANSI 837 standard format. The claims, automatically generated by the system, are reviewed manually by personnel for accuracy and consistency of coding. All billing is done in-house. Claims are submitted from the practice management system(s) to the payor, utilizing a
2

sections.title: Family Practice & Counseling Services Network 2026 Service Area Competition Application
sections.content: clearinghouse, which in turn forwards the ANSI 837 file to the payor. The submission of the file is monitored from FPCSN's system to the clearinghouse and from the clearinghouse to the payor, as is the file for a claims acknowledgement from the payor. FPCSN has implemented a full EDI process for claims and reimbursement (including claims payment posting for about 75% of all payments) and is in the process of having all reimbursements where applicable done through electronic funds transfer.

sections.title: b. Patient Payment Collection and Waiver
sections.content:
The FPCSN policy includes an Eligibility Determination process under which all patients have their eligibility for coverage checked prior to services being performed. Staff update each patient's insurance coverage at the time of the initial call for an appointment and also at the time of the reminder call. Upon the patient's arrival, the staff completes an eligibility verification. Some of the database systems in place are with the Pennsylvania Department of Human Services, the Medicaid MCO, Navinet, and various other Internet capabilities.
FPCSN collections procedures require that patients are asked for applicable co-pays and sliding fee amounts at the time of services. Patients who indicate that they cannot pay the amount owed may have a payment plan established or may have the nominal fee waived, depending on circumstances. For patients who do not make payments at the time of service, statements are presented to them at the time of service and education is provided on additional elements such as payment plans, grace periods, and prompt payment incentives. Attempts are made to collect the discounted fees, but no patients are refused care as a result of non-payment. If a patient states they cannot pay, the patient meets with the Social Services Manager to better understand and overcome the patient's circumstances. If the patient cannot pay, FPCSN policy is that the fee will be waived. All patients are seen for the visit regardless of ability to pay.

sections.title: BUDGET
sections.content: Please see the FPCSN Budget Narrative attached to this application.

sections.title: Board Authority
sections.content: The By-Laws for Family Practice & Counseling Services Network, Inc. are attached (Attachment 2). These By-Laws were developed this year and approved by the FPCSN Board on 4/16/25. The By-Laws indicate that the Board will meet Health Center Program requirements, including:

sections.title: a. Monthly Meetings
sections.content: The FPCSN By-Laws indicates in Article III Meetings that the Board is to meet at least once a month. Minutes are kept of all meetings and shared with Board members.

sections.title: b. Approving and Evaluating the CEO
sections.content: The By-Laws indicate in Article III Powers that the FPCSN Board is responsible for the selection and dismissal of the Chief Executive Officer.

sections.title: c. Approving Annual Budget and Applications
sections.content:
The By-Laws indicate in Article III Powers that the FPCSN Board has the responsibility to approve the FPCSN project budget annually and to approve applications made for health center funding.
3

sections.title: Family Practice & Counseling Services Network 2026 Service Area Competition Application
d. Approving Services Provided, Sites, and Hours of Operation
sections.content: The By-Laws indicate in Article III Powers that the FPCSN Board has the responsibility to approve the services, locations, and hours of operation of the health center sites.

sections.title: e. Evaluating Health Center Performance
sections.content:
The By-Laws indicate in Article III Powers that the FPCSN Board has the responsibility for evaluating the performance of the health center, including achievement of project objectives, service utilization patterns, quality of care, and efficiency and effectiveness.
f. Establishing Policy Related to Health Center Operations
The By-Laws indicate in Article III Powers that the FPCSN Board is responsible for establishing policy related to health center operations.
f/g. Establishes Policy Related to Health Center Operations and Assures Compliance The By-Laws indicate in Article III Powers that the FPCSN Board has the responsibility to assure that the health center operates in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local laws and regulations.

sections.title: Full Board Authority and Oversight
sections.content: The FPCSN Board elects its own members, as noted in Article III of the By-Laws. No individual, entity, or committee is noted as having authority or veto power over the Board and committees are specifically barred from amending the By-Laws or the Articles of Incorporation; selecting, approving, or dismissing Directors; or changing any resolution of the Board. There is no agreement or collaboration in place that restricts board authority.

sections.title: Compliance with Health Center Program Board Composition Requirements
sections.content:
The Family Practice & Counseling Services Network Board is vested with full authority and responsibility for the operations of the health center. The By-Laws indicate in Article III that the FPCSN Board is to include between 9 and 15 members. In Article III Composition, the By-Laws indicate that at least 51% of members are to be patients of the health center and should include representation from public housing. Article III Composition also indicates that of the non-patient members no more than 50% may derive more than 10% of their income from the health care industry and they should be representative of the community, including public housing, and should have expertise in community affairs, government, finance, legal affairs, and other commercial and/or social services in the community. Article III Composition indicates that no Director may be an employee of FPCSN or an immediate family member (i.e., spouse, child, parent, or brother or sister through blood, adoption, or marriage) of such an employee.
There are currently twelve members on the FPCSN Board, six of whom are FPCSN patients. One member is a representative of the Housing Authority and two others are or have been public housing residents. As noted on the Board list, Form 6A, the FPSN Board members have expertise in a number of fields, including law, public relations, education, community engagement, and healthcare operations and strategy. Three of the six non-patient members derive income from the health care industry.

3. Demonstration of Good Cause for Waiver (Demonstrate good cause for the waiver request by addressing the following areas)

3a. Provide a description of the population to be served and the characteristics of the population/service area that would necessitate a waiver. (This question is required if you answered Yes to question 1 and/or question 2b.)

4. Alternative Mechanism Plan for Addressing Patient Representation

Present a plan for complying with the intent of the statute via an alternative mechanism that ensures patient input and participation in the organization, as well as direction and ongoing governance of the health center. sections.title: * 11. 120 Day Compliance Achievement Plan Certification
sections.content: [ X ] By checking this box, I certify that if my organization is noncompliant with any Health Center Program requirements, in accordance with Section 330(e)(1)(B), I will submit for HRSA's approval within 120 days of release of the Notice of Award (NoA) a Compliance Achievement Plan to come into compliance. I acknowledge that areas of noncompliance will be documented through the carryover of any unresolved, existing condition(s) from the current period of performance and/or the placement of new condition(s) on the award based on the review of this application. I also acknowledge that all conditions on my award must be addressed within the timeframes and by the due dates specified on my Health Center Program NoA(s) and that the Compliance Achievement Plan I submit must align with such timelines.

sections.title: * 12. Uniform Data System (UDS) Report Certification
sections.content:
[ X ] By checking this box, I certify that I have reviewed the UDS Resources

I also acknowledge that failure to submit a complete report by the specified deadline may result in conditions or restrictions being placed on the Health Center Program award.

sections.title: * 13. Applicants for HP and RPH Funding: Supplement and Not Supplant Certification (New and competing supplement applicants only)
sections.content:
[_] Not Applicable. My organization is submitting a competing continuation application, or submitting a new or competing supplement application, but the organization is NOT requesting HP and/or RPH funding on the SF-424A.
[ X ] By checking this box, I certify that my organization will use HP and/or RPH grant funding to supplement and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations.
Describe, with specific examples, how you will use the requested federal funds to add new or expand existing services to the homeless population and/or residents of public housing within your service area, as well as how this is an increase or expansion of the services your organization was providing previously for these populations. (maximum 1,000 characters)
The health center has been providing health care services to residents of public housing and the surrounding communities in the service area for 33 years. Because the previous awardee changed ownership and relinquished the grant on 6/30/25, the FPCSN health center has established a new non-profit corporation and is applying as a new applicant for the area it has served for many years. In order to limit disruption in services to its patients, FPCSN has been able to keep all main sites open while providing the required services. However, in the last twelve months, because of the upheaval and uncertainty of the separation from the awardee and loss of FQHC status, many providers and other staff have left to seek positions elsewhere. Therefore, the requested federal funds will be used to add clinical provider staff to expand existing services so FPCSN can serve more patients with the service area.
